mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in housing units produced (HOUST) signals a strengthening demand for residential construction, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 70% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a weakening supply chain, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO fails to predict negative 20-day forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS fails to predict negative 60-day forward returns in > 80% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) signals a strengthening labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 70% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real GDP (GDPC1) signals a strengthening demand for goods and services, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 fails to predict positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) and a 1-month decline in housing units produced (HOUST) signals a weakening supply chain and a decline in residential construction, which is negatively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day forward returns in > 80% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) and a 2-month increase in the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals a tightening monetary cycle and a rise in inflation expectations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL fail to predict negative 20-day forward returns in > 70% of instances over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month increase in initial unemployment claims (UNRATE) and a 1-month increase in payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which is positively correlated with S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: UNRATE and PAYEMS fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns in > 80% of instances over any 3-year rolling window.
